response:
Yes we can check how long it took for udp packet to be sent to server like ping but only one direction

To do this we can use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class

We can create a UdpClient instance and send a packet to the server

We can then measure the time taken to send the packet using the DateTime.Now property

We can then calculate the time difference between the sending time and the receiving time

This will give us the time it took for the packet to be sent to the server

We can also handle any exceptions that may occur during the process

This method is similar to how ping works but only in one direction

This approach is efficient and can be used in a variety of applications

We can also use the NetworkInterface.GetIsNetworkAvailable method to check if the network is available before sending the packet

This ensures that the packet is only sent when the network is available

This method is reliable and can be used in a variety of applications

We can also use the NetworkInformation.GetNetworkInterface method to get the network interface information

This helps in determining the correct network interface to use
